key_points: Electric circuits must form a continuous conducting path between the positive and negative terminals of a power source
Circuit diagrams are simplified representations of electrical circuits using standard symbols
Electrical components perform specific roles such as providing energy, controlling current flow or converting electrical energy into light or heat
Multiple components can be connected in series or parallel to create different circuit behaviours
Safety and proper insulation are important when dealing with electrical circuits
examples: Series circuit example: Two bulbs connected in one path share the same current
Parallel circuit example: Bulbs connected in different branches glow independently
Circuit diagram symbols: Cell (long and short line), bulb (circle with filament), switch (break in line)
Testing conductivity: Using a bulb tester circuit to check if a material allows current flow
formulas: Electric circuit → Continuous closed path for current flow
Series connection → Components connected one after another in a single path
Parallel connection → Components connected in separate branches across the same source
exam_tips: Memorize common circuit symbols used in diagrams
Understand differences between series and parallel connections
Focus on reasoning questions about why a bulb glows or does not glow in a circuit
